title: Cisco VoIP - Telephone options for shared spaces
body: Shared Space Phone Options There are three main options that can be used for shared workspaces: Jabber, Extension Mobility, and Multiple Lines on a single phone. You can use more than one option. Please look through the descriptions of the items to find what would work best for you. Webex Webex is our software phone solution that allows you to use your campus phone number through your computer. If you are using Webex when you work from home, you can continue using it at the office. Check out our getting started page Advantages You will have the same setup at work and home. Do not have to type your NetID into the phone using a number pad Supports multiple phone numbers The application is used for your Webex Meetings as well Disadvantages Must have an internet connection. Action needed to use this option: Confirm with your IT staff that Jabber is installed on the computer you will be using. Request a Jabber account (if you do not have one already). Cisco VoIP Request Forms Extension Mobility Extension Mobility is a feature for Cisco phones that allows users to take their phone lines and device settings with them to Cisco phones across campus. Advantages Can use any UW-Madison Cisco VoIP phone and have your information on it Do not need to remember the headset. Disadvantages Entering your NetID into the phone using a number pad can be cumbersome. Only works for those in buildings with Cisco VoIP Telephones. Action needed to use this option: Find out the model of telephone you will be using. Fill out the Extension Mobility Account request form. Cisco VoIP - Extension Mobility Multiple Lines on a single phone The standard 7841 Cisco Phone has the capability to have 4 lines on it. If you are returning to the same shared space each time, this may be option. Each user could have their own line on a shared phone. Advantages Do not have to log into the phone each time Do not have to remember to bring a headset Disadvantages Only works for those returning to the same shared desk. Users may be distracted by incoming calls on the other lines of the phone. Limited number lines can be placed on the phone. Only works for those building with Cisco VoIP Telephones. Action needed to use this option: Talk to the person who manages the phones for your department. Ask them to request your line to be added to the phone near the workstation you will be using. If you are the person who manages the phones, please go to the Line Change Request. Cisco VoIP Request Forms If you need further help, please contact the DoIT Help Desk for assistance.
